text.title: Azure Architecture (Well-Architected + Landing Zones) Examples
text.summary: Worked end-to-end example applying this methodology to a concrete scenario.

example.title: Regulated SaaS adopts 3-MG hub-spoke
example.code:
Org: acme (200 devs, SOC2 + GDPR).
1. tenant-model → single Entra tenant; subs per env (dev/stage/prod) + per BU. 2. mg-hierarchy → root → platform / landingzones → corp / online (3 levels). 3. policy-initiative → baseline-initiative at platform MG: deny-public-ip, allowed-locations=[westeurope, northeurope], require-encryption. 4. identity → Entra ID + Managed Identity; PIM for Owner / User-Access-Admin / Contributor; activation requires approval + MFA. 5. network → hub-spoke (hub in connectivity sub); Private Endpoints + Private DNS for Storage / Key Vault / SQL. 6. bcdr → immutable Backup vault, retention 30d, RTO 4h, RPO 1h. Tested restore quarterly.
Result: Compliance audit passes; tenant ready for workload landing.
example.note: End-to-end trace through the 6-step procedure landing all 5 rules.
